source: Council for Medical Schemes — Circular 47 of 2022 (PMB DTPs)

algorithms.label: Algorithm A
algorithms.description: The medical scheme must fund prescribed minimum benefits at a Designated Service Provider (DSP) at cost — meaning no co-payment may be levied when the member uses the scheme's DSP. If the member chooses a non-DSP provider without a valid clinical reason, the scheme is obliged to fund only up to the DSP tariff rate, and the provider may charge the member the difference.
key_rules: No co-payment when member uses scheme DSP.
Non-DSP use: scheme pays DSP rate; provider may bill the balance.
Scheme must make DSP access reasonable (within 50 km or 1 hour travel).
Emergency exemptions apply — see Algorithm B provisions.
algorithms.applicability: Applies to most chronic (CDL) conditions and scheduled elective PMB procedures where a DSP network is in place.

algorithms.label: Algorithm B
algorithms.summary: Emergency PMB care at any provider
algorithms.description: The medical scheme must fund emergency PMB treatment at any registered service provider — regardless of whether that provider is a DSP. Once the member has been stabilised, the scheme may require transfer to a DSP for continued care. No co-payment may be levied for the emergency episode itself.
key_rules: Emergency treatment funded at any provider, at cost.
No co-payment for the acute emergency episode.
Post-stabilisation: scheme may require transfer to DSP.
Scheme must cover reasonable ambulance and transport costs.
Member cannot be denied emergency care due to scheme network rules.
algorithms.applicability: Applies to all life-threatening emergencies and acute presentations where immediate DSP access is not possible or safe.

algorithms.label: Algorithm C
algorithms.summary: PMB care at cost — any provider
algorithms.description: The medical scheme must fund prescribed minimum benefits at cost regardless of the service provider — DSP or non-DSP — and no co-payment may be levied. This is the most comprehensive funding obligation and applies where the scheme has not established a DSP network for the condition, or where the CMS has determined that DSP restriction is not appropriate.
key_rules: PMBs funded at actual cost — no co-payment, no shortfall.
Applies to both DSP and non-DSP providers.
Scheme cannot restrict provider choice for conditions governed by Algorithm C.
Applicable where no DSP network exists for the specific DTP.
Mental health PMBs are predominantly governed by Algorithm C.
algorithms.applicability: Applies to conditions where DSP restriction is not feasible or not approved by CMS — including many mental health, oncology, and rare-disease DTPs.
